front: Performer: first African-American prima donna at the Metropolitan Opera; made her Met debut in 1961 as Leonora in Verdi's Il Trovatore
Performer: best known for Verdi's Aida; first African-American lead at La Scala (1960); recorded Aida twice (with Solti in 1962 and Leinsdorf in 1970)
Performer: first Black singer in a televised opera (as Floria Tosca); made recordings of Tosca, Carmen, and Verdi's Requiem with Herbert von Karajan
Performer: starred in Porgy and Bess with then-husband William Warfield; that production chronicled in Truman Capote's The Muses Are Heard
Performer: opera stage debut as Madame Lidoine in the US premiere of Poulenc's Dialogues of the Carmelites; 1955 Carnegie Hall debut with pianist David Garvey
Performer: premiered Samuel Barber's Hermit Songs (1953) — a ten-song cycle setting Irish monks' poems — with Barber himself at the piano
Performer: premiered the title female role (Cleopatra) in Barber's Antony and Cleopatra at the new Met (1966) — considered a disastrous premiere; Barber's Despite and Still was also written for her
